hypothesis: Adopting a gradient, two-zone catalyst electrode that spatially decouples bicarbonate-to-i-CO2 formation from the CO2 reduction active sites—where the upstream region preferentially catalyzes i-CO2 generation from bicarbonate and the downstream region selectively reduces i-CO2 to CO—will increase reactor yield by boosting JCO while maintaining or increasing CO2 utilization (UCO2) compared with a uniform Ag gas-diffusion electrode under the same operating conditions.
